0101.title: CACR APPOINTED IN WRITING

quotes.text: Casualty Assistance Command Representative (CACR). SNCO or above, down to the Battalion/Squadron/I&I level, responsible for ensuring the unit is adhering to the policies of the Casualty Assistance Program and remains up to date on all significant changes to the program stemming from congressional legislation, benefit enhancements, or revised HQMC policies.

Casualty Assistance Command Representative (CACR). As tasked in this Order, every unit, down to the Battalion/Squadron/I&I level will have a Casualty Assistance Command Representative assigned in writing. The mission of this individual will be to ensure that the unit is adhering to the policies of the Casualty Assistance Program and remains up to date on all significant changes to the program stemming from congressional legislation, benefit enhancements, or revised HQMC policies.

regulatory.summary: Every unit down to the Battalion, Squadron, and I&I level is required to have a SNCO or above designated as CACR through a written appointment. The CACR is responsible for ensuring command compliance with the Casualty Assistance Program and awareness of all policy changes.

0103.title: SOES SGLI BENEFICIARY ELECTION

quotes.text: Commanders shall ensure ALL Marines who have not made an election in SOES make an election and 'Confirm and Certify' before exiting SOES, to ensure SGLI beneficiary designations are stored in the system. Commanders shall ensure their Marines make an election in SOES upon the following occasions: a. Joining the unit, b. Review/update of the Record of Emergency Data (RED), c. Reenlistment, d. At the request of the Marine, e. Upon request for accelerated benefits, f. Prior to deployment, g. Upon activation/mobilization or permanent change of station and prior to appellate leave or separation.

Commands are directed to establish procedures to ensure maximum compliance is attained and maintained at all times. This requirement shall be inspected during inspections utilizing the Casualty 3040 Functional Area Checklist. New join audits do not meet the criteria for updating SOES, as only the Record of Emergency Data (RED) is updated.

regulatory.summary: Commands are required to establish and maintain a formal process ensuring every Marine makes a confirmed election in SOES at each of the seven required triggering events. Compliance must be tracked and maintained at all times. A new join RED audit alone does not satisfy SOES requirements.

0105.title: TELEPHONIC CONTACT AND PCR SUBMISSION TIMELINE

quotes.text: When a reportable casualty occurs, it is the responsibility of the casualty's command to notify MFPC and the MCOC, and to submit a Personnel Casualty Report (PCR). In the case of a reportable casualty, the command will notify MFPC and the MCOC telephonically prior to release of the PCR.
Timeliness. Report as soon as possible but no later than one (1) hour after learning of the incident.

Voice Report. Commands will contact the MCOC at 1-866-476-2669 and MFPC at 1-800-847-1597 immediately upon learning a reportable casualty has occurred providing, at a minimum, the casualty's name, social security number and basic circumstances surrounding the casualty incident.

regulatory.summary: Upon learning of a reportable casualty, the command must immediately make a voice report to both MCOC and MFPC. Telephonic contact with MFPC must occur before the PCR is released. The PCR must be submitted no later than one hour after learning of the incident.

quotes.text: Casualty Type - As appropriate: Hostile. Non-hostile. Pending (Determination Pending). Casualty Status - As appropriate: D-Deceased. M-Missing (will not be used unless an official declaration has been made by SECNAV or his designee, DC, M&RA). N-Not Wounded in Action. P-Duty Status Whereabouts Unknown (DUSTWUN). V-Very Seriously Ill or Injured. S-Seriously Ill or Injured. I-Ill or Injured but Not Seriously. H-Wounded in Action. Casualty Category - As appropriate: A-Accident. B-Besieged. C-Captured. D-Detained. H-Homicide. I-Illness. G-Missing in Action. K-Killed in Action. L-Beleaguered. M-Self-inflicted. N-Interned. P-Pending. R-Returned to Military Control. T-Terrorist Activity. V-Hostile Death. W-Wounded in Action.

0107.title: DUSTWUN PCR PROCEDURES AND TIMELINES

quotes.text: Any Marine suspected to be a casualty, who is not present or appears to be missing (i.e., whereabouts are unknown, the disappearance appears to be involuntary, and the Marine is not known to be UA), and whose status initially is so uncertain that the commander must gather the facts to make a proper status determination, must be reported as DUSTWUN, per chapter 3. Additionally, Marines thought to be UA/Deserter in an Official Combat Zone or Operation should be reported as DUSTWUN. Make no SRB or unit diary entries at this time.

Once reported as DUSTWUN, the commander with special courts-martial convening authority, having jurisdiction over the Marine will convene a fact-finding body to investigate the circumstances surrounding the disappearance. The maximum time a Marine may remain in a nonhostile DUSTWUN status is 10 days from the date of disappearance.

Subsequent to submission of the Personnel Casualty Report (PCR) placing the Marine in a DUSTWUN status, the commander will submit a daily supplemental PCR to MFPC that includes the extent of the search, any progress noted, and other pertinent information. Daily supplemental PCRs are required, unless not feasible for security reasons, and will continue until the search has been discontinued.

regulatory.summary: A DUSTWUN declaration is required when a Marine's disappearance appears involuntary and facts are insufficient for a definitive status determination. An initial PCR must be submitted immediately. Daily supplemental PCRs follow until resolution. A nonhostile DUSTWUN status has a maximum duration of 10 days. Missing status requires a formal declaration by SECNAV or DC M&RA.

0108.title: PROGRESS REPORTS FOR HOSPITALIZED MARINES

quotes.text: Injured/Ill Cases. A supplemental/progress PCR will be submitted at reasonable intervals, depending upon prognosis, but at least every week until the individual's category has been downgraded below seriously ill/injured (SI), is sent on convalescent leave, or is returned to duty. 'Returned to duty' means that the Marine is no longer required to attend any physical therapy or outpatient care, as stated by the attending physician, for the injury described in the Initial PCR. Supplemental PCRs will be generated by the parent command except in the following circumstances: 1. If the Marine is located in a theatre of operations, Supplemental PCRs will be generated by the Forward Command Element. 2. For Marines medevaced out of theatre of operations, Supplemental PCRs will be generated by Patient Affairs Teams (PATs) until the Marine has been returned to the parent command or administratively attached to another unit.
Incident Investigations. Generate supplemental PCRs at least every 30 days from the date of initial report to provide a status of the investigation until completed.

regulatory.summary: For SI and VSI hospitalized Marines, progress/supplemental PCRs are required at least weekly until the casualty is downgraded below SI, placed on convalescent leave, or returned to duty. Updates to a casualty's medical condition or location are reported via Progress Report (PROG) type in DCIPS-Forward, not a Supplemental Report (SUPP).

0109.title: SPECIAL PATIENT (SPECPAT) CASES

quotes.text: Special Patient (SpecPat). The category of a casualty applicable when the casualty's illness or injury is not otherwise reportable but involves: (1) incurable or terminal diseases (Acquired Immune Deficiency Syndrome (AIDS) is not classified as a terminal disease); (2) diagnosed psychotic conditions requiring hospitalization; (3) injuries such as loss of sight or limb, paralysis, or permanent and unsightly disfigurement of a portion of the body exposed to public view; or (4) attempted suicide, verified by medical authority.

SpecPat. When a Marine makes a suicide attempt, as verified by medical authority, notification will be made as follows: 1. the Marine notifies the PNOK; requests notification not be made; or 2. if the Marine does not indicate a clear intent, or is not capable of making notification, MFPC will make telephonic notification to the PNOK.

regulatory.summary: Special Patient is a distinct casualty category triggered by four specific conditions not otherwise reportable. A PCR must be submitted when a SpecPat condition is identified. AIDS is explicitly excluded from the terminal disease classification. SpecPat suicide attempt cases have a unique notification protocol where the Marine is given the opportunity to notify the PNOK first.

0110.title: ACCELERATED BENEFIT OPTION (ABO)

quotes.text: SGLI Accelerated Benefits Option (ABO). The ABO permits terminally ill Marines or spouses, insured under the SGLI or FSGLI program, access to the death benefits of the policy before death. The Marine or spouse must have a valid written prognosis from a physician of nine months or less to live. Only the insured Marine may apply and receive proceeds from the ABO on behalf of him/herself or their spouse. The Marine may receive up to 50 percent, or a lesser amount in increments of $5,000, of the face value of insurance coverage in a lump-sum payment.

The ABO permits Marines and spouses insured under Servicemembers' Group Life Insurance (SGLI) and Marines insured under Veterans' Group Life Insurance (VGLI) access to death benefits of the policy before death. a. A Marine is eligible to receive accelerated benefits only after a physician has diagnosed the insured, in writing, with a prognosis of 9 months or less to live. b. The amount of the accelerated benefit available to the Marine is up to 50 percent of the face value of the Marine's or spouse's insurance coverage. If the Marine elects less than the maximum, the amount requested must be in increments of $5,000. c. The portion of the face value of insurance which is not paid in a lump sum as accelerated benefits remains payable to the designated beneficiary upon death of the insured.
The insured Marine is the only individual that may apply for accelerated benefits. No other individual may apply on the Marine's behalf.
Effective Date. The election to receive the accelerated benefits becomes effective at the time the Marine cashes or deposits the accelerated benefits check.

regulatory.summary: The ABO allows terminally ill Marines or spouses insured under SGLI or FSGLI to access up to 50% of their policy's face value before death. Eligibility requires a written physician prognosis of nine months or less to live. Only the insured Marine may apply. The election becomes effective when the Marine cashes or deposits the payment.

0111.title: NOK NOTIFICATION FOR ILL/INJURED MARINES

quotes.text: Manner of Notification. Notification will be made on reportable casualties as outlined in subparagraphs (a) through (e). a. Active Duty (1) Deceased, Duty Status Whereabouts Unknown (DUSTWUN), and Missing. The primary NOK (PNOK) and secondary NOK (SNOK), as defined in Chapter 1, will be notified in person. (2) Ill or Injured (a) Hostile Not Seriously Injured (NSI) - Wounded in Action, Seriously Ill or Injured (SI), and Very Seriously Ill or Injured (VSI). The PNOK and SNOK will be notified telephonically by MFPC. Following telephonic notification, a Casualty Assistance Calls Officer (CACO) may be assigned at the request of the commander or as directed by MFPC. (b) SpecPat. When a Marine makes a suicide attempt, as verified by medical authority: 1. the Marine notifies the PNOK; requests notification not be made; or 2. if the Marine does not indicate a clear intent, or is not capable of making notification, MFPC will make telephonic notification to the PNOK.

SI, VSI, and hostile NSI require telephonic notification by MFPC to both PNOK and SNOK. SpecPat (suicide attempt) notification begins with the Marine - MFPC makes telephonic notification only if the Marine cannot or does not clearly indicate a preference.

0112.title: INVITATIONAL TRAVEL AUTHORIZATIONS - BEDSIDE AND BURIAL

quotes.text: Invitational Travel Orders (ITO) to Bedside. Per reference (q), ITO(s) may be issued to not more than three family members for round-trip transportation and per diem to visit an active duty Marine who is seriously ill or injured or in a situation of imminent death, whether or not electrical brain activity exists or brain death is declared, and who is hospitalized in a medical facility anywhere in the world. a. Classifications Eligible. Active Duty. b. Beneficiary Determination. Family members are the Marine's spouse, children (including step, adopted, and illegitimate children), siblings, and parents. c. Claim Procedures. Request for ITO to bedside should be submitted to MFPC. ITO(s) will be approved, funded, and issued by MFPC. Commanders are not authorized to utilize command funds to issue an ITO.

Invitational Travel Orders (ITO) to Burial. Travel to and from the burial site and 2 days per diem are authorized. A Government Transportation Request (GTR) should be utilized whenever possible. a. Death Classification Eligible. ITO(s) are authorized for eligible family members of active duty and retired Marines placed on the T/PDRL who die within 120 days of separation while retained in a medical treatment facility as a result of service connected disability. b. Beneficiary Determination. Per reference (q), ITOs may be issued to the spouse, spouse's parents, children, parents, siblings, and Person Authorized to Direct Disposition (PADD) to attend the burial or memorial service. c. Claim Procedure. All ITOs will be approved and/or produced by MFPC.

Burial ITOs are limited to travel plus two days per diem.

0113.title: ITAs TO UNIT MEMORIAL SERVICES

quotes.text: Beneficiary Determination. Per reference (q), ITOs may be issued to the spouse, spouse's parents, children, parents, siblings, and Person Authorized to Direct Disposition (PADD) to attend the burial or memorial service (if remains are not recovered).

Department of Defense policy now allows for travel of eligible family members to attend a unit memorial service held in memory of their deceased Marine. Travel and transportation allowances include travel to and from the unit memorial service plus two days of per diem at the memorial site.

Only one unit memorial service is recognized for government-funded transportation and allowances. Travel and transportation allowances are limited to a two year period following the loss of the Marine. Memorial locations are limited to the Continental United States, Alaska, Hawaii and U.S. Territories. The location can also be the last permanent duty station of the Marine. Family members are not authorized travel to memorial services conducted in a theater of combat operations.
Travel is limited to the following individuals: Spouse, Children, Parents, Marine's Siblings, Spouse's Parents and the Person Authorized to Direct Disposition of Human Remains (PADD).
The command hosting the memorial will generate Invitational Travel Orders for eligible family members.

Travel to memorials in a theater of combat operations is not authorized. Reimbursement covers travel plus two days per diem.

0114.title: REQUIRED DOCUMENTS EMAILED TO MFPC IN DEATH CASES

quotes.text: Acquire copies of the following: last signed RED; last signed SGLV 8286; page one of the Record of Military Processing (DD Form 1966/1), page one of the Military Enlistment/Reenlistment Form (DD Form 4/1) for enlisted; Appointment Acceptance and Record (NAVMC 763) for officers; page three (chronological record) from the Service Record Book (SRB)/Officer Qualification Record (OQR) and email, or fax, copies to MFPC. Updates to the RED via Marine Online (MOL) will supersede a previous signed RED. Additionally, a review of MCTFS is required to determine other changes to the RED.

Other Required Documents. In addition to the initial voice report and preparation/submission of the PCR, the following applicable documents from the Marine's SRB/OQR must be faxed to MFPC immediately upon learning of a Marine's death: a. A copy of the Marine's latest signed Record of Emergency Data (RED). As this is the only document used by MFPC to determine the appropriate beneficiary of certain death benefits, e.g., death gratuity and pay arrears, it is vital that the signed RED be provided. b. A copy of the Marine's latest signed SGLV 8286 and SGLV 8285, if applicable. c. A copy of the Marine's DD Form 1966/1 (1st page). d. A copy of the Marine's DD Form 4/1. e. A copy of the Marine's NAVMC 00003 (leave papers), PCSO, or TADO in all cases involving Marines who die while in a leave, PCS, or TAD status. f. A copy of the Marine's most recent NAVMC 118(3), Chronological Record page. g. A copy of orders in cases involving Reserve Marines who die during or en route to or from an AT, ADT, IDT or ADSW period. h. In cases involving the death of an individual in the DEP, a copy of all enlistment documents.

0118.title: DEATH BENEFITS FOR SURVIVORS

quotes.text: Death Gratuity (DG). DG is a lump-sum payment intended to help assist eligible beneficiary(ies) with immediate living expenses. Death Classification Eligible: Active Duty, T/PDRL who die within 120 days of separation and death is Service connected. Beneficiary Determination: Marines can designate a beneficiary to receive up to 100% of the DG in increments of 10%. If no designation, DG is paid in order: (1) surviving spouse, (2) children, (3) surviving parents, (4) executor or administrator, (5) other next of kin. Claim Procedure: CACO provides DG beneficiary's SSN to MFPC. A pre-populated DD Form 397 and EFT will then be faxed or emailed to the CACO.

Arrears of Pay (AP). All pay and allowances terminate on the date of death. AP is the final settlement of a Marine's pay account. AP includes any unpaid compensation, unused leave, and reenlistment bonuses (if applicable). Beneficiary Determination: AP is paid to the surviving beneficiary designated on the Marine's most recently signed RED. Claim Procedure: The beneficiary must complete and submit the SF 1174, Claim for Unpaid Compensation of Deceased Member of the Uniformed Services.
General. Figure 7-1, grouped by classification, is provided as a quick reference tool for the most common benefits and entitlements.

regulatory.summary: The CACR must explain four core death benefits. Death Gratuity is designated on the RED in 10% increments. Arrears of Pay beneficiary is determined by the RED. SGLI beneficiary is determined by the SGLV 8286. Figure 7-1 serves as the at-a-glance reference covering all benefits by casualty classification.

0119.title: BENEFITS FOR T/PDRL AND 120-DAY MARINES

quotes.text: Retired. For casualty reporting purposes, retired Marines are separated into the following classifications: Marines on the Temporary/Permanent Disability Retired List (T/PDRL) that die within 120 days after separation and all other retired Marines.

Retired Marines placed on the T/PDRL that die within 120 days due to their Service connected disability, are eligible to receive many of the same benefits and entitlements as active duty Marines with the following exception: (1) Burial allowances (Marine must be retired/retained). (2) Basic Allowance for Housing (BAH) (retired Marines are not entitled).
Death Classification Eligible. Active Duty, T/PDRL who die within 120 days of separation and death is Service connected.

T/PDRL deaths within 120 days are reportable and require a PCR.

0120.title: UNIT-LEVEL COMMANDER CONDOLENCE LETTERS

quotes.text: When and By Whom. (1) Active Duty Marines. A condolence letter will be written to the appropriate next of kin (NOK) according to the Marine's dependency status; if the Marine is married, to both the spouse and parents (if the parents are separated or divorced, letters will be sent to both parents); if the Marine is divorced or single with children, a condolence letter will be written to the children (in care of the guardian) and the parents. (a) The Marine's immediate commander will prepare a letter within 5 working days (combat committed organizations, within 10 days) of the Marine's death or notification of death. (b) When a Marine dies away from his/her parent command either in TAD or PCS status, the commander of the unit from which the Marine had just been detached is responsible to write the letter of condolence.
All condolence letters will be carefully reviewed at the highest level of command prior to release for mailing.
A copy of each letter sent to the family of a deceased Marine will be attached to the closed service records.

Letters are addressed based on the Marine's dependency status. All letters must be reviewed at the highest command level before release, and copies are retained with the closed service records.

0121.title: PCS TRAVEL ORDERS FOR FAMILY MEMBERS

quotes.text: Eligibility. The following dependents may be eligible for travel allowances: 1. A dependent of a Service member who dies while entitled to basic pay (37 U.S.C. 481). 2. A dependent, without regard to command-sponsorship, who receives an official notice that the Service member on active duty is officially reported as: a. Dead. b. Injured or ill and the anticipated period of hospitalization or treatment is expected to be prolonged. c. Absent for a period of 30 or more days in a missing status (37 U.S.C. 484).

When a Service member who is entitled to basic pay dies on or after January 6, 2006, dependent PCS travel and transportation allowances are authorized. The dependent has 3 years, beginning on the Service member's date of death to choose an HOS. An extension to this time period, not to exceed a total of 6 years, may be authorized or approved through the Secretarial Process. Per diem is authorized for a dependent traveling under this subparagraph.

Escort. An escort to accompany an eligible dependent for travel under this paragraph may be authorized when the AO or commanding officer determines that dependent travel is necessary and the dependent is incapable of traveling alone due to age, mental or physical incapacity, or other extraordinary circumstances. 1. An escort may be authorized for the Service member's dependent for travel performed no later than 1 year after the Service member dies. Escort travel is not authorized after the 1-year limit is reached, and the time limit cannot be extended, regardless of the circumstances.

Survivor/Family Member Travel. Per the JFTR, approved family members, residing with the Marine, are authorized transportation at Government expense to the Marine's home of record, or any other location within the CONUS. Death Classifications Eligible: Active Duty, T/PDRL who die within 120 days of separation due to service connected disability. Claim Procedures: Travel orders should be issued citing the same appropriation data used in travel for separating Marines. Method of Settlement: Transportation includes shipment of household goods covered under the Marine's allowance.

regulatory.summary: Family members of deceased active duty Marines are authorized PCS travel at government expense to the Marine's home of record or another CONUS location. The dependent has three years from the date of death to initiate travel, extendable up to six years through the Secretarial Process. Household goods shipment is included. Escort authorization expires one year after the date of death with no exceptions.

0122.title: PRELIMINARY INQUIRIES AND COMMAND INVESTIGATIONS

quotes.text: Conduct preliminary inquiries into the injury/illness of a Marine in accordance with reference (i). When warranted, command investigations will be conducted.

Conduct preliminary inquiries into the deaths of Marines, regardless of the circumstances, and including hostile deaths, in accordance with reference (i). When warranted, command investigations will be conducted.

Incident Investigations. Preliminary inquiries will be conducted into the deaths/injuries/illnesses of Marines in accordance with reference (i). When warranted, command investigations will be conducted. An initial PCR will include detailed information pertinent to the investigative process. Supplemental PCRs will be generated every 30 days to provide MFPC and the next of kin with a status of investigations until completed.

Purpose. A preliminary inquiry serves as an analytical tool to help a commander determine whether an investigation is warranted and, if so, how it should be conducted. A preliminary inquiry is not intended to develop extensive findings of fact.

Generally, a commander is responsible for initiating preliminary inquiries into incidents occurring within, or involving personnel of, the command. The reporting command of a member who is injured or dies during permanent-change-of-station transfer shall ensure that appropriate inquiries are conducted.

Time limitations. Generally, the preliminary inquiry should be completed within three calendar days of the commander learning of the incident in question. The commander may grant extensions as necessary on a case-by-case basis.

regulatory.summary: Preliminary inquiries are mandatory for all deaths, injuries, and illnesses of Marines regardless of circumstances, including hostile deaths. The commanding officer initiates within three calendar days. Supplemental PCRs are required every 30 days until a command investigation is completed.

0123.title: FINAL SUPPLEMENTAL PCRs AND ENDORSEMENTS AFTER INVESTIGATION

quotes.text: Incident Investigations. Generate supplemental PCRs at least every 30 days from the date of initial report to provide a status of the investigation until completed. Within 24 hours following the completion of an investigation, generate a supplemental PCR providing the completion date, findings and date of delivery to the next of kin. For Friendly Fire incidents, units will update the 'inflicting Force' block as appropriate.

MFPC is the single activity responsible for tracking and archiving all injury and death investigations, limited to executive summaries. Completed command investigations will be forwarded to the General Court-Martial Convening Authority (GCMCA), Attn: Staff Judge Advocate (SJA).

Delivery to NOK. Prior to the release of the investigative findings to the NOK, the investigating unit will notify MFPC of its completion in order to arrange for proper coordination for delivery. Graphic photographs are to be separately wrapped and labeled. Similar procedures should be placed upon autopsy reports and other materials containing graphic details of injury, wounds, and mutilation. The releasing authority will coordinate with the Casualty's commander to ensure that both a PCR and a voice report are given to MFPC to alert them and document the release of the investigation. Normally, the CACO will make delivery, but there may be reasons (technical subject matter or personal friendships) for another individual to be assigned the task.

regulatory.summary: Upon completion of a command investigation, the unit must generate a final supplemental PCR within 24 hours providing the completion date, findings, and date of delivery to the NOK. The unit must notify MFPC before releasing the investigation to the NOK. Both a PCR and a voice report to MFPC are required to document the release. Graphic materials must be separately wrapped and labeled.

0124.title: ARCHIVING PCRs FOR SIX YEARS

quotes.text: PCR Requirements. The PCR is used to report casualty information and is used by the CACO to inform the casualty's next of kin of the details surrounding the incident. The report is administrative rather than operational; it should be sent immediately, in an unclassified manner, to the DC M&RA (MF) directly from the activity submitting the report. The report must be accurate and contain only verified information. PCRs will be maintained by reporting commands for six years in accordance with Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) requirements.
Maintain Personnel Casualty Reports (PCRs) for six years in accordance with the requirements of the Freedom of Information Act (FOIA), reference (d).

regulatory.summary: All PCRs - initial, supplemental, progress, and final - must be maintained by the reporting command for a minimum of six years. This is a Commanding Officer-level responsibility established under FOIA. All PCR types submitted through DCIPS-Forward are subject to this retention requirement.

0125.title: ONLINE CACO REPORT SUBMISSION AND WEEKLY COMMANDER BRIEFING

quotes.text: Initiate the On-Line Casualty Assistance Calls Report (1770) within 72 hours of assignment. Use of on-line report is mandatory.
Brief Commander on a weekly basis utilizing the Casualty Assistance Call Report (1770) as to the status of the casualty assistance provided to the next of kin.

Complete and submit to MFPC, an Initial Casualty Assistance Call Report (1770) (via the Manpower Portal) within 30 days of assignment. This report is extremely valuable in documenting assistance provided to the NOK, addressing problem areas and recommendations concerning the Casualty Assistance Calls Program. The online report should be updated daily to report actions completed. The CACO Report will be completed, in its entirety, and submitted to MFPC for filing in the Casualty's case file.
An interim report is due to MFPC thirty (30) days from the date of assignment as CACO.

The final report is due sixty (60) days from the date of assignment as CACO. If extenuating circumstances exist that preclude the completion of the report within the established time frame, contact MFPC to request an extension.

regulatory.summary: Upon CACO assignment, the officer must INITIATE the Online CACO Report within 72 hours via the Manpower Portal (https://www2.manpower.usmc.mil/cacov3/). The completed initial report must be SUBMITTED to MFPC within 30 days. A final report is due within 60 days. The report is updated daily as actions are completed. The CACR briefs the unit commander weekly on CACO assistance status. The 72-hour initiation and 30-day submission are two separate, sequential requirements.
